title: Confusing dual representation of routing success in IncomingResult::RoutingResponse
description:
File: rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/bridge.rs:116-120

The RoutingResponse struct uses Option<RoutingErrorCode> where success can be represented two ways:

```rust
RoutingResponse {
    request_id: u32,
    error: Option<RoutingErrorCode>,  // None = success, Some(None) = also success??
}
```

And RoutingErrorCode has a None variant (line 62):
```rust
pub enum RoutingErrorCode {
    None,  // = 0, means no error
    NoRoute,
    ...
}
```

This creates ambiguity:
- `error: None` means success (no error code present)
- `error: Some(RoutingErrorCode::None)` also means success

The code handles this at line 230-235:
```rust
error: if error_code == RoutingErrorCode::None {
    None
} else {
    Some(error_code)
}
```

But callers might not expect this normalization, and the dual representation is confusing.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. Rename RoutingErrorCode::None to RoutingErrorCode::Success for clarity
2. Use a dedicated RoutingResult enum: enum RoutingResult { Success, Error(RoutingErrorCode) }
3. Add doc comments explaining the normalization
